content.full_text:
CITY OF ORANGE

CITY ATTORNEY—(714) 532-0351                                    FAX (714) 532-0437

                                                    May 14, 1990


Mr. John Rothman, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE:  Request for Legal Advice:  Mayor Don E. Smith

Dear Mr. Rothman:

     Following up our discussion, I am submitting on behalf of
the City of Orange Mayor Don E. Smith, this request for written
advice pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b).  (Mayor Smith's
authorization is attached.)

                         Factual Background

     In 1985 the City Council received a recommendation to
authorize a parking study from the Old Towne Steering Committee,
a Council appointed citizens committee to review the parking
conditions in the Old Towne area of Orange. The purpose of the
study was to determine the existing and long term parking demand
of the area as projected for the year 2010.

     The 100 S. Orange site designated in the study was currently
being used as a municipal parking lot. The study recommended
that this existing parking lot be expanded by the construction of
a parking structure. The proposed construction would involve a
structure of no more than 35 feet in height, as required by the
area's zoning restrictions. The structure would add to the
existing 88 stalls an additional 212 parking stalls. The study
further recommended the need to improve the 100 S. Orange parking
lot was motivated by the following factors:

     1)  Additional parking will be necessary to accommodate
anticipated land used. However, the projected future land use is
not an increase in development but a conversion from commercial
-retail, such as furniture, and antique stores to a mixed
residential office-professional use.


_________________________________________________________________________

ORANGE CIVIC CENTER  •  300 E. CHAPMAN AVE.  •  ORANGE, CA 92666-1591  •  P.O. BOX 449

Letter to Mr. Rothman
Re: Request for Advice - Mayor Smith
May 11, 1990
Page Two

     2)     Improving the existing parking lot would facilitate and
enhance the parking opportunity for locating Senior Citizen
Housing Development in the Old Towne Area.

     The conclusions regarding the 100 S. Orange parking lot
improvement have been affirmed by the City Council. The next
step is to undertake decisions on the design and development of
the improvement.

     Mayor Don E. Smith is presently refraining from any
participation in this parking improvement both at the Council
subcommittee and on the City Council level based on his concern
of a possible financial effect from the improvement on certain
real estate that he owns.

     As to these properties, for clarity I have arranged below
the properties by their proximity to the boundary of the intended
public parking improvement along with each properties' charac-
teristics, income stream and parking availability. Please note
that the distance has been calculated based on the number of feet
traveled by a straight line. If the distance is measured by
traversing the streets, the distance should be increased by
approximately 200 feet for each property:

     1)    254, 254 1/2, 256 1/2 North Grand Street

          a)    1026 feet

          b)    40 year old house approximately 700 square feet
                converted to a duplex. 256 1/2 small garage
                apartment.

          c)    $450 rent for each

          d)    Off street parking available only to residents of
                neighborhood

     2)    531, 541 East Chapman Avenue

          a)    1270 feet

          b)    two story, 7000 foot office-professional building
                major tenants: attorneys, accountants, private
                investigator

          c)    two to five year leases

          d)    free parking lot which satisfies City parking
                requirements for size of building

Letter to Mr. Rothman
Re: Request for Advice - Mayor Smith
May 11, 1990
Page Three

3) 605 East maple, 219 East Cleveland

a) 2005 feet

b) 70 year old two story single family residence

c) $700 rent

d) attached one car garage and off street parking

4) 702 East Chapman

a) Mayor Smith's residence

5) 123-125 South Harwood

a) 1700 feet

b) 700 foot single family residence and apartment
over garages

c) $700 rent for each

d) rear garages and off street parking

ISSUE PRESENTED

The issue to be resolved is whether Mayor Smith is precluded
from participation regarding the 100 South Orange parking
improvement due to his ownership of any one of the above listed
properties. To assist you, as discussed, please find enclosed
the opinion of MacMillin & Associates, independent real estate
appraisers. They have opined that the proposed public
improvement will not impact the market value of the above
referenced properties.

In analyzing this issue, it appears that the determination
of material financial effect under Section 18702.3 (a)(3) of the
California Administrative Code as applied to each individual
property referenced above, any participation on this parking
improvement would not have the requisite material financial
effect. The reason for this conclusion is the increase has a
minimal impact on the overall parking availability in the Old
Towne area. Further, between the Mayor's commercial property and
the improvement there is a substantial number of parking
opportunities either off street or in lots.

Letter to Mr. Rothman
Re: Request for Advice - Mayor Smith
May 11, 1990
Page Four


     Your assistance in providing a written advice letter on this
issue would be appreciated.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   Luis A. Rodriguez
                                   Sr. Assistant City Attorney

AUTHORIZATION TO SEEK ADVICE OF
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION


I DON E. SMITH, Mayor of the City of Orange, California, do
hereby authorize Luis A. Rodriguez, as Senior Assistant City
Attorney of the City of Orange, to seek advice from the
California Fair Political Practices Commission on my behalf
in order to properly determine my ability to vote pursuant to
the law of the state of California concerning matters arising
in the improvement and operation of an existing public
parking facility located at 100 South Orange Street, Orange,
California. I further certify that my home mailing address
is 702 E. Chapman Ave., Orange, California 92666. I have
reviewed material pertaining to my interests as set forth in
that letter by Luis A. Rodriguez dated as of 5/[illegible]
, and confirm that the statements made therein with respect
to my interests are accurate, and that I know of no sources
of income to me which are required to be disclosed other than
those set forth in the letter by Senior Assistant City
Attorney Luis A. Rodriguez.

Date: ___5-11-90___ ___________________________
                     Mayor, City of Orange


/kdb

Mac MILLIN & ASSOCIATES                                    REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

3631 South Harbor Blvd.                           Telephone (714) 957-2565
Suite 125                                         FAX (714) 755-5777
Santa Ana, CA. 92704


                                                           May 10, 1990


Mr. Luis A. Rodriguez
Senior Assistant City Attorney
City of Orange
300 East Chapman Avenue
Orange, California 92666

Re:     1.  254, 254 1/2 North Grand Street
        2.  531, 541 East Chapman Avenue
        3.  605 East Maple Avenue, 219 East Cleveland Street
        4.  702 East Chapman Avenue
        5.  123-125 South Harwood
            Orange, California

Dear Mr. Rodriguez:

In accordance with your request, we have completed a preliminary investigation to determine if the market value of the
above referenced real properties will be enhanced by $10,000 or
more, or the rental value by $1,000 or more per 1/2 month period
from the operation of the proposed parking structure project to
be located at 100 South Orange Street, Orange, California.

It is our opinion that the market value and the rental value of
the above referenced properties will not be impacted by the
development of the parking structure, beyond the normal market
appreciation.

The approximate distance from the proposed parking structure to
the above referenced properties is noted as follows:

                    ADDRESS                         DISTANCE IN FEET

        1.  254, 254 1/2 North Grand Street              1,026
        2.  531, 541 East Chapman Avenue                 1,270
        3.  605 East Maple Avenue,
            219 East Cleveland Street                    2,005
        4.  702 East Chapman Avenue                      1,700
        5.  123-125 South Harwood                        1,700

As noted above, none of the properties are located within 300
feet of the proposed parking structure.

Mac MILLIN & ASSOCIATES     REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

Mr. Luis A. Rodriguez
Senior Assistant City Attorney

May 10, 1990
Page 2

We do not have any past, present or future interest in the sub-
ject properties.  Thank you for the opportunity of serving you
in this matter.

Respectfully submitted,

Paul G. MacMillin, MAI

Susan B. Napier, Associate

Mc. MILLIN & ASSOCIATES          REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

Page 3                                                    May 10, 1990

                         APPRAISAL CERTIFICATION

     The undersigned do hereby certify that, except as otherwise noted
in this appraisal report:

     To the best of our knowledge and belief, the statements of fact
contained in this appraisal report and upon which the opinions,
analyses and conclusions expressed herein are based, are true and
correct.

     The appraisal report identifies all of the limiting conditions
(imposed by the terms of our assignment or by the undersigned)
affecting the analyses, opinions and conclusions contained in
this report.

     Employment in and compensation for making this appraisal are in
no way contingent upon the value reported and we certify that we
have no interest (or the stated interest), either present or con-
templated, in the subject property. We have no personal interest
or bias with respect to the subject matter of the appraisal
report or the parties involved.

     No one other than the undersigned prepared the analyses,
opinions, or conclusions concerning real estate that are set
forth in this appraisal report.

     The appraisal analyses and opinions were developed in conformity
with, and is subject to, the requirements of the Code of Profes-
sional Ethics and Standards of Professional Practice of the
American Institute of Real Estate Appraisers. The use of this
letter is subject to the requirements of the American Institute
of Real Estate Appraisers relating to review by its duly
authorized representatives.

     The American Institute of Real Estate Appraisers conducts a
voluntary program of continuing professional education for its
designated members. MAI or RM members who meet the minimum stan-
dards of this program are awarded periodic educational certifica-
tion. I, Paul MacMillin, am currently certified under this
program.

     We, the undersigned, have personally inspected the exterior of
the above referenced real property.


_________________________              _________________________
Paul G. MacMillin, MAI               Susan B. Napier, Associate

Mc MILLIN & ASSOCIATES    REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

Page 4                                                May 10, 1990

ASSUMPTIONS AND LIMITING CONDITIONS
As a clarification of the appraisal problem, the following explanation of conditions upon which our analysis is premised is
herein offered.

1. No investigation of title to the property has been made, and
   the premises are assumed to be free and clear of all encumbrances, leases, use restrictions, easements, cases or actions pending, except those specifically discussed in this
   report.  Title is assumed to be good and marketable, and that
   the property is under responsible ownership, competent management and available for its highest and best use.

2. No survey, legal or engineering analysis of this property has
   been made by the appraisers.  We assume no responsibility for
   any condition not readily observable from our customary inspection of the premises, and that there are no hidden or unapparent conditions of the property, subsoil or structures
   that render it more or less valuable, except as noted here.

3. It is assumed that there is full compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local environmental regulations
   and laws unless noncompliance is stated, defined, and considered in this appraisal report.

4. It is assumed that all applicable zoning and use regulations
   and restrictions have been complied with, unless a nonconformity has been stated, defined, and considered in this appraisal
   report.

5. It is assumed that all required licenses, certificates of occupancy, consents, or other legislative or administrative
   authority from any local, state, or national government or
   private entity or organization have been or can be obtained or
   renewed for any use on which the value estimate contained in
   this report is based.

This appraisal report has been prepared with the following limiting conditions:

1. The distribution, if any, of the total valuation in this
   report between land and improvements applies only under the
   stated program of utilization.  The separate allocations for
   land and buildings must not be used in conjunction with any
   other appraisal and are invalid if so used.

2. Possession of this report, or a copy thereof, does not carry
   with it the right of publication.  It may not be used for any
   purpose by any person other than the party to whom it is addressed without the written consent of the appraisers.  We do

Mac MILLIN & ASSOCIATES      REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

Page 5                                                    May 10, 1990

     not authorize out of context quoting, nor partial reprinting
of the report.   In the event this report is placed in the
hands of a third party, it is requested that such party be
made cognizant of any and all limiting conditions resulting
from the basis of our employment and discussions related
thereto, as well as those set forth herein.

3. The submission of this report constitutes completion of the
service authorized.  It is submitted upon the condition that
the client will provide the appraisers customary compensation
relative to any subsequent required deposition, conferences,
additional preparation or testimony.

4. The appraisers respectfully request that neither all nor part
of the contents of this report shall be disseminated to the
public through advertisement, public relations, news, sales,
or other media, without the written consent and approval of
the author, particularly as to valuation conclusions, the
identity of the appraisers, or any reference to the American
Institute of Real Estate Appraisers or to the MAI or RM desig-
nations.

5. This report is based, in part, upon information assembled from
a wide range of sources and therefore, the incorporated data
cannot be guaranteed in any fashion.  An impractical and un-
economic expenditure of time would be required in attempting
to furnish unimpeachable verification as to engineering, con-
struction and structural data, title and use restrictions,
zoning, and proposed public and private projects in the area.

6. The appraisers reserve the right to make such adjustments to
the valuation herein reported, as may be required by con-
sideration of additional data or more reliable data that may
become available.

7. That no opinion is intended to be expressed for matters which
require legal expertise or specialized investigation or
knowledge beyond that customarily employed by real estate ap-
praisers.

8. The maps, photographs and exhibits included in the report are
for illustration only as an aid in visualizing matters dis-
cussed herein.  They should not be considered as surveys, or
relied upon for any other purpose, nor should they be removed
from, reproduced or used apart from this report.

9. Unless otherwise stated in this report, the existence of haz-
ardous material, which may or may not be present on the
property, was not observed by the appraisers.  The appraisers
have no knowledge of the existence of such materials on or in

Max MILLIN & ASSOCIATES          REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS

Page 6                                                    May 10, 1990

     the property. The appraisers, however, are not qualified to
detect such substances. The presence of substances such as
asbestos, urea-formaldehyde foam insulation, or other poten-
tially hazardous materials may affect the value of the
property. The value estimate is predicated on the assumption
that there is no such material on or in the property that
would cause a loss in value. No responsibility is assumed for
any such conditions, or for any expertise or engineering
knowledge required to discover them. The client is urged to
retain an expert in this field.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor Don E. Smith is required to recuse himself from voting on a proposed public parking structure improvement project at 100 South Orange Street due to his ownership of nearby residential and commercial properties that may be affected by the improvement.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Includes attached authorization from Mayor Smith and MacMillin & Associates appraisal report. Document is formatted with old-style letterhead and formatting typical of 1990 correspondence.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The issue to be resolved is whether Mayor Smith is precluded from participation regarding the 100 South Orange parking improvement due to his ownership of any one of the above listed properties.
embedding.first_500_words: CITY OF ORANGE CITY ATTORNEY—(714) 532-0351 FAX (714) 532-0437 May 14, 1990 Mr. John Rothman, Esq. Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J. Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 RE: Request for Legal Advice: Mayor Don E. Smith Dear Mr. Rothman: Following up our discussion, I am submitting on behalf of the City of Orange Mayor Don E. Smith, this request for written advice pursuant to Government Code § 83114(b). (Mayor Smith's authorization is attached.) Factual Background In 1985 the City Council received a recommendation to authorize a parking study from the Old Towne Steering Committee, a Council appointed citizens committee to review the parking conditions in the Old Towne area of Orange. The purpose of the study was to determine the existing and long term parking demand of the area as projected for the year 2010. The 100 S. Orange site designated in the study was currently being used as a municipal parking lot. The study recommended that this existing parking lot be expanded by the construction of a parking structure. The proposed construction would involve a structure of no more than 35 feet in height, as required by the area's zoning restrictions. The structure would add to the existing 88 stalls an additional 212 parking stalls. The study further recommended the need to improve the 100 S. Orange parking lot was motivated by the following factors: 1) Additional parking will be necessary to accommodate anticipated land used. However, the projected future land use is not an increase in development but a conversion from commercial -retail, such as furniture, and antique stores to a mixed residential office-professional use. _________________________________________________________________________ ORANGE CIVIC CENTER • 300 E. CHAPMAN AVE. • ORANGE, CA 92666-1591 • P.O. BOX 449 Letter to Mr. Rothman Re: Request for Advice - Mayor Smith May 11, 1990 Page Two 2) Improving the existing parking lot would facilitate and enhance the parking opportunity for locating Senior Citizen Housing Development in the Old Towne Area. The conclusions regarding the 100 S. Orange parking lot improvement have been affirmed by the City Council. The next step is to undertake decisions on the design and development of the improvement. Mayor Don E. Smith is presently refraining from any participation in this parking improvement both at the Council subcommittee and on the City Council level based on his concern of a possible financial effect from the improvement on certain real estate that he owns. As to these properties, for clarity I have arranged below the properties by their proximity to the boundary of the intended public parking improvement along with each properties' charac- teristics, income stream and parking availability. Please note that the distance has been calculated based on the number of feet traveled by a straight line. If the distance is measured by traversing the streets, the distance should be increased by approximately 200 feet for each property: 1) 254, 254 1/2, 256 1/2 North Grand Street a) 1026 feet b) 40 year old house approximately 700 square feet converted
embedding.summary: City Attorney Luis A. Rodriguez submits a request for FPPC legal advice on behalf of Mayor Don E. Smith regarding whether the Mayor must recuse himself from participating in decisions on a proposed parking structure improvement in Orange's Old Towne area due to his ownership of several nearby properties. The request includes an independent real estate appraisal opinion concluding that the parking improvement will not impact the market value of the Mayor's properties.
